cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a search group formed by some American scientists in 1975. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the details from the passage. The passage mentions that in 1975, American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. The passage says that some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a very ugly head on a four-meter neck. Because of this, many people began to believe in the monster again. However, the text also states that even today, we can't be certain if the monster exists. Looking at the options now: Option A: found the monster itself. But the passage says they took pictures that seemed to show a monster, not that they actually found the monster. So A is probably incorrect. Option B: found a huge cave underwater. The text mentions that a huge underwater cave was found, but that was in the part about the 1961 group using underwater cameras, not the American scientists in 1975. So B is not correct here. Option C: believed there wasn't any monster at all. The passage says that after the American scientists took the pictures, many people believed in the monster again.
